title: Maintain spend pools and transaction pool assignment
preconditions: The Ledger is readable; required pool identities can be created without tying them to one bank account, instrument, cardholder, or category.
postconditions: Pool and category actuals remain exact and independently explainable; no account, instrument, cardholder, or description silently determines the pool.
main_flow: 1. The caller creates, lists, renames, archives, or reactivates stable spend pools, including pools representing company-paid and personal-after-tax spending. 2. Tally validates unique active identity and historical references. 3. The owner assigns or corrects one active pool for a spend transaction with reason and idempotency identity, or leaves it explicitly unassigned. 4. Tally appends pool-assignment history independently from category and other dimensions. 5. Refunds follow the original current pool; transfers contribute zero; voids, supersessions, and corrections change active actuals atomically. 6. Queries return exact pool totals and pool-by-category detail plus explicit unassigned values. 7. Archive prevents new assignment while preserving history.
alt_flows: 1a. Pool identity conflicts -> reject without mutation
2a. Referenced pool is archived -> reject new assignment until reactivated
3a. Assignment replay is identical -> return the original result
3b. Conflicting reuse, inactive transaction, or stale assignment -> return conflict and preserve current state
4a. Assignment write fails -> retain the prior pool state
5a. Refund, transfer, correction, or lifecycle effect cannot reconcile exactly by pool/category -> fail closed
6a. No pool is assigned -> include the transaction in explicit unassigned-pool actuals rather than omitting it
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"The owner needs to separate company-paid discretionary or personal-expense spending from personal after-tax spending and inspect exact actuals.","status":"draft"}
